parameterdescription.para: RNG seed. Two generators with different seeds produce uncorrelated noise streams.
Per-component (Re, Im) standard deviation. Must be ≥ 0.

simplesect.para: Heap-allocated state, or NULL on allocation failure.

detaileddescription.para: Free all resources. NULL is a no-op.
Reset RNG to the seed supplied at create time.
Return the current amplitude (per-component std dev).
Set amplitude without disturbing RNG state.
Reseed the RNG and reset state.

briefdescription.para: Conservative upper bound on generate() output size.

detaileddescription.para: Returns 65536. The Python extension uses this for the initial buffer allocation; the buffer grows on demand if n > 65536.

briefdescription.para: Generate n complex CF32 AWGN samples.

simplesect.para: n (always; generate produces exactly n samples).

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t seed, float amplitude, size_t n, float complex *out)
memberdef.name: awgn

briefdescription.para: One-shot AWGN generation — no persistent state required.

simplesect.para: DP_OK on success, DP_ERR_MEMORY on allocation failure.

briefdescription.para: AWGN generator: xoshiro256++ RNG + Box-Muller transform.

sect3.title: Algorithm
sect3.para: Two paths share the same logical algorithm but differ in width:

For each output sample, consume two 64-bit RNG words: u1 = (top-24-bits(word0) + 1) × 2^-24 ∈ (0, 1] (log-safe) idx = top-16-bits(word1) ∈ [0, 65535] r = amplitude × sqrt(−2 × ln u1) (Box-Muller radial) out = r × cos(idx) + j × r × sin(idx) (96 dBc LUT phase)

sect3.title: Scalar path
sect3.para: One xoshiro256++ state (4 × uint64). Two next() calls per sample.

sect3.title: AVX-512 path
sect3.para: Eight independent xoshiro256++ streams stored in vs[0..3][0..7] (word × stream layout). Each stream occupies one 64-bit lane of a __m512i register, giving 8 independent outputs per SIMD step.

listitem.para: Two xs8() calls produce 8 u1 and 8 idx values.
_mm256_i32gather_ps reads sin and cos from the 65536-entry LUT.
Two _mm256_storeu_ps interleave re/im directly into the output.

sect3.title: Sin/cos LUT
sect3.para: 2^16 single-precision entries, identical layout to lo_core: lut[i] = sin(2π·i / 65536) lut[i + LUT_QTR] = cos(2π·i / 65536) (uint16 wrap, no branch)
SFDR ≈ 96 dBc — sufficient for noise generation at any SNR of practical interest.

sect3.title: References
sect3.para: xoshiro256++: Blackman & Vigna, ACM TOMS 47(4), 2021. SplitMix64: Steele et al., PLDI 2014. _ZGVdN8v_logf: glibc libmvec, 8-wide single-precision log (AVX2).
